claims.claim: Historical Fern CLI bare-array pagination limitation is no longer an active blocker because the required SDK generator is local; wrapper pagination helpers remain the supported public surface.
claims.boundary: No active generator blocker. Generated list methods still need to preserve page/page-size request shapes consumed by iterAll, iterPages, and paginate.

projection.summary: Historical Fern CLI bare-array pagination limitation is no longer an active blocker because the required SDK generator is local; wrapper pagination helpers remain the supported public surface.
projection.impact: No active generator blocker. Generated list methods still need to preserve page/page-size request shapes consumed by iterAll, iterPages, and paginate.
projection.mitigation: Keep pagination wrappers tested and keep local generator comparison/drift checks active.
projection.closureGate: No closure planned; keep as historical evidence unless the pagination helper surface is intentionally redesigned.

claims.claim: The historical Fern addon-token workaround is retired; local generated auth types now model exactly one token mode.
claims.boundary: A regression could reintroduce workaround casts or allow callers to send both Clockify auth headers.

projection.summary: The historical Fern addon-token workaround is retired; local generated auth types now model exactly one token mode.
projection.impact: A regression could reintroduce workaround casts or allow callers to send both Clockify auth headers.
projection.mitigation: Keep generated HeaderAuthProvider auth options mutually exclusive and keep createClockifyClient auth tests active.
projection.closureGate: No closure planned; keep discrepancy evidence and SDK auth tests as regression coverage.

claims.claim: Live tests intentionally mutate Clockify state and must run only against a sacrificial sandbox workspace.
claims.boundary: Live proof is high value but unsafe against customer workspaces.

projection.summary: Live tests intentionally mutate Clockify state and must run only against a sacrificial sandbox workspace.
projection.impact: Live proof is high value but unsafe against customer workspaces.
projection.mitigation: Require explicit env vars and document sandbox-only usage before running live gates.
projection.closureGate: Keep live proof receipts showing sandbox cleanup; never generalize live gates to customer workspaces.

claims.claim: Packages are published to npm under the unofficial @apet97 scope; publication stays tag-gated CI, so npm publication is not the default path for routine changes and the publish manifest fields + prepublishOnly gates remain load-bearing.
claims.boundary: Publishing is a deliberate tag push (wrapper-v*/cli-v*/mcp-v*); the prepublishOnly gates and tag-vs-version guards are the safety rails. Re-publishing on every change is not the workflow.

projection.summary: Packages are published to npm under the unofficial @apet97 scope; publication stays tag-gated CI, so npm publication is not the default path for routine changes and the publish manifest fields + prepublishOnly gates remain load-bearing.
projection.impact: Publishing is a deliberate tag push (wrapper-v*/cli-v*/mcp-v*); the prepublishOnly gates and tag-vs-version guards are the safety rails. Re-publishing on every change is not the workflow.
projection.mitigation: Keep release/support policy, package contract, and CI contract aligned with the tag-gated publish stance.
projection.closureGate: Only publish after explicit maintainer approval plus perfect-full, pack-smoke, and release/support proof.

claims.claim: Tag-triggered npm release workflows publish all three packages on prefixed tags (wrapper-v*/cli-v*/mcp-v*); the maintainer reviewed and enabled them (release-please files the wrapper version-bump PR; release.yml/ci-cli-release.yml/ci-mcp-release.yml each publish on a tag whose version matches its package.json).
claims.boundary: Release automation still requires explicit maintainer approval per publish; local packable readiness is not the same as permission to publish. The tag-vs-version guard remains load-bearing.

projection.summary: Tag-triggered npm release workflows publish all three packages on prefixed tags (wrapper-v*/cli-v*/mcp-v*); the maintainer reviewed and enabled them (release-please files the wrapper version-bump PR; release.yml/ci-cli-release.yml/ci-mcp-release.yml each publish on a tag whose version matches its package.json).
projection.impact: Release automation still requires explicit maintainer approval per publish; local packable readiness is not the same as permission to publish. The tag-vs-version guard remains load-bearing.
projection.mitigation: Keep the no-default-publish policy, package contracts, CI contract, and release-decision planner visible; do not run or modify release automation casually. release-please can file PRs after enabling the repo-level `can_approve_pull_request_reviews` setting.
projection.closureGate: Maintainer reviewed release.yml + release-please.yml on 2026-05-28; on 2026-06-28 the maintainer enabled tag-triggered npm publish for all three packages under the unofficial @apet97 scope (CLI/MCP scaffolds flipped from inert to cli-v*/mcp-v* publishers; SDK moved from bare v* to wrapper-v* tags). The tag-vs-version guard remains load-bearing.

claims.claim: Generated code is a replaceable lower layer, not the product surface users should depend on directly.
claims.boundary: Direct imports from generated internals make generator replacement and OpenAPI regeneration brittle.

projection.summary: Generated code is a replaceable lower layer, not the product surface users should depend on directly.
projection.impact: Direct imports from generated internals make generator replacement and OpenAPI regeneration brittle.
projection.mitigation: Keep generated-edit, generator-independence, dependency-boundary, and SDK public API gates active.
projection.closureGate: No closure planned; this is a permanent architecture constraint.

claims.claim: Live read-only proof established that the expense route ignores start/end while honoring pagination; SDK, CLI, and MCP now share one bounded client-side filter contract.
claims.boundary: Date-filtered reads can require multiple API pages and may stop at the configured bound, so consumers must inspect the warning and continuation metadata.

projection.impact: Date-filtered reads can require multiple API pages and may stop at the configured bound, so consumers must inspect the warning and continuation metadata.
projection.mitigation: Keep listExpensesFiltered shared across CLI/MCP, preserve the typed double-nested envelope, and retain the bounded scan plus client-side warning.
projection.closureGate: Closed 2026-07-19 by the sanitized Task 2 live receipt, focused wrapper/CLI/MCP tests, consumer-cast budget, operation-parity drift, and contract gates.

of reconstructed rest objects, and JavaScript last-write object-spread reconstruction while ordinary non-rest spreads remain outside the special seam, shared pre-materialization work/alternative
